{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Tomb |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size = Unknown |Construction = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Tomb |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size = Unknown |Construction = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size = Unknown |Construction = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. revision.comment: fixing unknown parameters from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Tomb |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tomb|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|keyhole-shaped|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|sword|ja|大刀|12=draft}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Muraita Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Muraita Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:村居田古墳 up to rev 102590891
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 村居田古墳 |画像 = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘残丘（息長陵付属地） |別名 = 広姫息長陵古墳{{Sfn|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}/皇后塚{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}} |所属 = 長浜古墳群 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[米原市]]村居田字北屋敷 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 24|緯度秒 = 5.28 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 20|経度秒 = 10.90 |ISO = JP-25 |形状 = （一説）[[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 不明 |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]中葉-後半 |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[広姫]] |出土品 = 宝冠・[[大刀]]・鏡・[[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「息長陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shiga |ラベル = 村居田古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[広姫]]息長陵 拝所}}{{small|元禄期の出土遺物の埋納地。明治期に円丘が築かれて陵に治定。}}]] '''村居田古墳'''（むらいだこふん）は、[[滋賀県]][[米原市]]村居田にある[[古墳]]。形状は一説に[[前方後円墳]]。長浜古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、一部が[[宮内庁]]により「'''息長陵'''（おきながのみささぎ）」として第30代[[敏達天皇]][[皇后]]の[[広姫]]の[[陵墓|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|荒陵山光運寺}}{{small|本堂左側に村居田古墳残丘。}}]] 滋賀県北東部、湖北地方の横山丘陵の北端部付近に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。一帯では長浜茶臼山古墳（[[長浜市]]東上坂町）・垣籠古墳（長浜市垣籠町）などとともに長浜古墳群（横山北部古墳群）を構成する{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。『坂田郡志』によれば、[[元禄]]9年（[[1696年]]）の荒陵山光運寺の本堂改築の際に発見されて石槨内から石棺等が出土したが、それらは別の場所に埋納された（現在の息長陵本地）{{Sfn|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。この時に墳丘の大部分が削平されており{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}、墳丘の残丘部分も現在は息長陵付属地として[[宮内庁]]の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は削平のため明らかでないが、[[前方後円墳]]の可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。周辺では[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]）が検出されている{{Sfn|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、施設内部には[[家形石棺]]が据えられていた{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。石棺古図によれば縄掛突起4個を有する阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（[[阿蘇ピンク石]]）製の石棺とされ、同製の石棺としては東限になる可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。また『輿地志略』によれば、元禄の発見の際には石棺とともに宝冠・[[大刀]]・鏡なども出土したが、これらの副葬品も石棺とともに埋納されたという{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。 この村居田古墳は、石棺・出土埴輪より[[古墳時代]]中期中葉-後半の[[5世紀]]中葉-後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。長浜古墳群では長浜茶臼山古墳に後続し、垣籠古墳に先行する首長墓に位置づけられる{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。被葬者は明らかでなく、前述のように現在は宮内庁により[[広姫]]（[[6世紀]]中頃）の陵に治定されているが、築造年代は活動年代に合わずその可能性は低いとされる{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。横山丘陵南端部では[[息長氏]]（おきながうじ）の首長墓群に比定される息長古墳群の築造も知られるが、長浜古墳群は息長古墳群の前代首長墓群に位置づけられるため、息長氏の発展を巡る当時の歴史的背景を考察する説も挙げられている{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[元禄]]9年（[[1696年]]）、光運寺本堂の改築工事の際に石槨・石棺等の発見。領主検分ののち出土遺物を堀居氏邸内に埋納{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）10月、[[教部省]]による現地検察。石槨石材・石棺蓋について模写{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1874年]]（明治7年）5月、教部省により息長陵に考証{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。 * [[1875年]]（明治8年） ** 7月、陵掌・陵丁の設置{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 ** 9月、遺物埋納地に新たに円墳を築き御陵とする太政官決定{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。 * [[1877年]]（明治10年） ** 10月、兆域確定時に堀居氏が邸地の一部を献納{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 ** 11月、御陵造営{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1883年]]（明治16年）10月、旧御陵地も宮内省管轄下に治定{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1884年]]（明治17年）12月、陵掌・陵丁の廃止、守部1名の設置{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1895年]]（明治28年）9月、石柵設置{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 == 被葬者 == 村居田古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第30代[[敏達天皇]][[皇后]]の'''[[広姫]]'''（ひろひめ）の[[陵墓|陵]]に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。広姫について、『[[日本書紀]]』では[[敏達天皇]]4年（[[575年]]?）11月に崩御したとするが、陵の記載はない{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠墓の「息長墓」として記載され、[[近江国]][[坂田郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西1町・南北1町で守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。その後、息長墓の所在に関する所伝は失われ、後世の坂田郡内には「皇后塚」と称される古墳が数ヶ所存在していた{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。村居田古墳もその「皇后塚」の1つになる{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。 前述のように、村居田古墳では元禄9年（1696年）に石棺等が出土したのち堀居氏邸内に埋納され、1874年（明治7年）5月には[[教部省]]により息長陵に考証、[[1875年]]（明治8年）7月には掌丁付置が命じられたが、上述の経緯を踏まえて同年9月に遺物埋納地に円丘が築かれてそれが息長陵に定められた{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。その後、[[光運寺]]本堂南側の古墳残丘は息長陵付属地と定められ、陵の参道に囲い込まれている{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}（この息長陵付属地を遺物埋納地に比定する解釈もある{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}）。ただし、前述のように村居田古墳の築造年代は広姫から遡る時期であるため、現在では広姫の陵である可能性は低いとされる{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=石田茂輔|year=|chapter=息長陵|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|息長陵（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=息長墓|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 25 滋賀県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=菅谷文則|authorlink=菅谷文則|year=2002|chapter=広姫息長陵古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=辻川哲朗|year=2006|chapter=米原市村居田古墳の再検討|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-bunkazai.jp/download/kiyou/19_tsujikawa.pdf 紀要 第19号]}}|publisher=財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|辻川哲朗|2006年}}}} - リンクは滋賀県文化財保護協会。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=水谷千秋|authorlink=水谷千秋|year=2013|chapter=|title=継体天皇と朝鮮半島の謎（文春新書925）|publisher=[[文藝春秋]]|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|水谷千秋|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[広姫]] {{デフォルトソート:むらいたこふん}} [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:米原市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[村居田古墳]] to [[Muraita Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 村居田古墳 |画像 = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘残丘（息長陵付属地） |別名 = 広姫息長陵古墳{{Sfn|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}/皇后塚{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}} |所属 = 長浜古墳群 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[米原市]]村居田字北屋敷 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 24|緯度秒 = 5.28 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 20|経度秒 = 10.90 |ISO = JP-25 |形状 = （一説）[[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 不明 |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]中葉-後半 |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[広姫]] |出土品 = 宝冠・[[大刀]]・鏡・[[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「息長陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shiga |ラベル = 村居田古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[広姫]]息長陵 拝所}}{{small|元禄期の出土遺物の埋納地。明治期に円丘が築かれて陵に治定。}}]] '''村居田古墳'''（むらいだこふん）は、[[滋賀県]][[米原市]]村居田にある[[古墳]]。形状は一説に[[前方後円墳]]。長浜古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、一部が[[宮内庁]]により「'''息長陵'''（おきながのみささぎ）」として第30代[[敏達天皇]][[皇后]]の[[広姫]]の[[陵墓|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|荒陵山光運寺}}{{small|本堂左側に村居田古墳残丘。}}]] 滋賀県北東部、湖北地方の横山丘陵の北端部付近に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。一帯では長浜茶臼山古墳（[[長浜市]]東上坂町）・垣籠古墳（長浜市垣籠町）などとともに長浜古墳群（横山北部古墳群）を構成する{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。『坂田郡志』によれば、[[元禄]]9年（[[1696年]]）の荒陵山光運寺の本堂改築の際に発見されて石槨内から石棺等が出土したが、それらは別の場所に埋納された（現在の息長陵本地）{{Sfn|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。この時に墳丘の大部分が削平されており{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}、墳丘の残丘部分も現在は息長陵付属地として[[宮内庁]]の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は削平のため明らかでないが、[[前方後円墳]]の可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。周辺では[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]）が検出されている{{Sfn|広姫息長陵古墳群（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、施設内部には[[家形石棺]]が据えられていた{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。石棺古図によれば縄掛突起4個を有する阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（[[阿蘇ピンク石]]）製の石棺とされ、同製の石棺としては東限になる可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。また『輿地志略』によれば、元禄の発見の際には石棺とともに宝冠・[[大刀]]・鏡なども出土したが、これらの副葬品も石棺とともに埋納されたという{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。 この村居田古墳は、石棺・出土埴輪より[[古墳時代]]中期中葉-後半の[[5世紀]]中葉-後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。長浜古墳群では長浜茶臼山古墳に後続し、垣籠古墳に先行する首長墓に位置づけられる{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。被葬者は明らかでなく、前述のように現在は宮内庁により[[広姫]]（[[6世紀]]中頃）の陵に治定されているが、築造年代は活動年代に合わずその可能性は低いとされる{{Sfn|息長墓（平凡社）|1991年}}。横山丘陵南端部では[[息長氏]]（おきながうじ）の首長墓群に比定される息長古墳群の築造も知られるが、長浜古墳群は息長古墳群の前代首長墓群に位置づけられるため、息長氏の発展を巡る当時の歴史的背景を考察する説も挙げられている{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013年|pp=76-97}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[元禄]]9年（[[1696年]]）、光運寺本堂の改築工事の際に石槨・石棺等の発見。領主検分ののち出土遺物を堀居氏邸内に埋納{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）10月、[[教部省]]による現地検察。石槨石材・石棺蓋について模写{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 * [[1874年]]（明治7年）5月、教部省により息長陵に考証{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。 * [[1875年]]（明治8年） ** 7月、陵掌・陵丁の設置{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 ** 9月、遺物埋納地に新たに円墳を築き御陵とする太政官決定{{Sfn|息長陵（国史）}}。 * [[1877年]]（明治10年） ** [[1895年]]（明治28年）9月、石柵設置{{Sfn|辻川哲朗|2006年}}。 == 被葬者 == 村居田古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第30代[[敏達天皇]][[皇后]]の'''[[広姫]]'''（ひろひめ）の[[陵墓|陵]]に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Tomb |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|村居田古墳|version=102590891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Muraita Kofun]] [[en:Muraita Kofun]] [[ja:%E6%9D%91%E5%B1%85%E7%94%B0%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:米原市の歴史
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Tomb |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|村居田古墳|version=102590891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|村居田古墳|version=102590891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Muraita Kofun]] [[ja:%E6%9D%91%E5%B1%85%E7%94%B0%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] {{Commons category|Muraida Kofun}} [[ja:村居田古墳]]

While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Maibara
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}}
