Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = 35.4014667 |coordinates = 136.3363611 |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|村居田古墳|version=102590891|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Muraita Kofun]] It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A stone coffin was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a stone coffin and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of stone coffin fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. [[sn:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48748983]] Wikidata name: Muraida Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. [[co:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48748983]] Wikidata name: Muraida Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] |burial goods = Crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], mirror, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara]], Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, [[:en:大刀|sword]], and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] {{Kofun navbar}} Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}.
