Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword|1=大刀}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=sword|1=大刀}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|qid=Q1224104|lt=sword|1=大刀}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|qid=Q1224104|lt=sword|1=大刀}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Shosuke Ishida|year=|chapter=Okinaga Tomb|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten (Showa built)|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|ref={{Harvid|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fuminori Sugaya|year=2002|chapter=Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} * However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. [[co:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Muraida Kofun]][[ja:村居田古墳]] [[ja:村居田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Muraida Kofun |image = [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, zankyu.jpg|280px]]
Remains of the mound (Auxiliary area of Okinaga Tomb) |alternate_name = Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Kofun{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}/Kōgōzuka{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}} |part_of = Nagahama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture, Muraida Aza Kitayashiki |coordinates = {{Coord|35.401467|N|136.336361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-25 |type = (Hypothetical) {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} |map_size = Unknown |built = Mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}} |burial goods = Crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|qid=Q1224104|lt=sword|1=大刀}}, mirror, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = Designated as "Okinaga Tomb" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Muraida Kofun |type = |map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Okinaga-no-Misasagi, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Shrine}}{{small|Relics reburied in Genroku era; mound constructed and Historic Site1 as a tomb in Meiji era.}}]] '''Muraida Kofun''' (村居田古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture. Its type is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, and it is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster. The actual individual buried in the tomb remains unclear. However, part of the site has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, empress of Emperor Bidatsu, the 30th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == [[File:Kouunji (Maibara), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple}}{{small|Mound remains are adjacent to the temple's main hall.}}]] Muraida Kofun is situated in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture, near the northern end of the Yokoyama Hills in the Hokoku region{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. It is part of the Nagahama Kofun Cluster (Yokoyama Northern Kofun Cluster), which includes tombs such as the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun and the Kakigome Kofun in Nagahama City{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. According to the *Sakata District Gazetteer* (*Sakata-gunshi*), the mound was discovered in Genroku 9 (1696) during renovations to the main hall of Araryōzan Kōun-ji Temple. A Sarcophagus was unearthed from the mound but later reburied at the current tomb site (Historic Site1 as Okinaga Tomb). During this discovery, much of the original mound was destroyed{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}, and the remaining parts of the mound are now managed by the Imperial Household Agency. No full-map_size investigations have been conducted due to this Historic Site{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The mound's exact type is unclear due to erosion, but it is hypothesized to be {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. Surrounding the site, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (cylindrical haniwa) have been found{{Sfn|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Inside the mound, a {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of Aso welded tuff (known as Aso Pink Stone) was discovered. This coffin, with its four protrusions for rope attachments, represents the easternmost example of such coffins{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. burial goods recorded during the Genroku excavation included a crown, {{ill|大刀|en|大刀|qid=Q1224104|lt=sword|1=大刀}}, and mirrors. However, these were also reburied alongside the coffin{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The tomb is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the mid-Kofun period. It follows the Nagahama Chausuyama Kofun chronologically and predates the Kakigome Kofun{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76–97}}. While it has been Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|qid=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, this attribution is considered unlikely due to discrepancies in the timeline{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == Excavation History == * Genroku 9 (1696): Discovery of a Sarcophagus and associated burial goods during temple renovations. Relics reburied at the Hori Residence{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5): Inspection by the Ministry of Education, with sketches made of Sarcophagus fragments{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. * 1874 (Meiji 7): Official Historic Site as Okinaga Tomb by the Ministry of Education{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1875 (Meiji 8): Mound construction at reburial site, officially recognized as a tomb{{Sfn|Okinaga Tomb (Kokushi)}}. * 1883 (Meiji 16): Former burial site brought under Imperial Household Agency jurisdiction{{Sfn|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}. Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Hirohime Okinaga Tomb Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsuro Tsujikawa|year=2006|chapter=Reevaluation of Muraida Kofun in Maibara City|title=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association Annual Report Vol. 19|publisher=Shiga Historic Site Protection Association|ref={{Harvid|Tetsuro Tsujikawa|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Chiaki Mizutani|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Mystery of Emperor Keitai and the Korean Peninsula|publisher=Bunshun Shinsho|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|Chiaki Mizutani|2013}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Muraida Kofun}} *
